name: Caparroso and Peñaflor Castle Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – circular desde Caparroso hasta el Castillo de Peñaflor
description: Explore the unique semi-desert landscape of Bardenas Reales on this 24.5-mile mountain bike route to Peñaflor Castle from Caparroso.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Caparroso are set within the unique semi-desert landscape of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park in Navarre, Spain. This region features eroded clay, gypsum, and sandstone formations, creating a distinctive terrain with "cabezos" (earthen castles) and ravines. The area offers a network of dirt tracks and technical sections, providing varied riding experiences. Pine forests also feature in some routes, complementing the arid landscapes. Best mountain bike trails around Caparroso The most popular mountain bike trail is Caparroso and Peñaflor Castle Loop, a 24.5 miles (39.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route leads through the Bardenas Reales to the historic Peñaflor Castle. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castildetierra Hill – Cabezo de las Cortinillas loop from El Ferial, a moderate 34.0 miles (54.7 km) path. This trail explores the iconic semi-desert landscape, passing by the striking Castildetierra formation. Local mountain bikers also love the Ferial Reservoir – View of the Bardenas Reales loop from El Ferial, a 22.1 miles (35.7 km) trail leading through the Bardenas Reales, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Mountain biking around Caparroso is defined by semi-desert landscapes, "cabezos" formations, and varied dirt tracks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Caparroso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Caparroso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Caparroso?
A: The mountain bike trails around Caparroso are primarily set within the unique semi-desert landscape of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. This means you'll encounter eroded clay, gypsum, and sandstone formations, creating a distinctive terrain with 'cabezos' (earthen castles) and ravines. Many routes feature dirt tracks and technical sections, with some also passing through pine forests, offering a varied riding experience.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Caparroso area?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Caparroso, catering to various skill levels. This includes 5 easy routes, 33 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, ensuring options for everyone from beginners to experienced riders.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes near Caparroso?
A: Yes, there are several easier routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10465332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferial Reservoir loop from El Ferial&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 9.2 km trail that takes less than an hour to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or scenic views I can see while mountain biking in Caparroso?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. You can encounter iconic formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt;, a striking 'cabezo' that is a natural monument. Historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3848041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor Castle&lt;/a&gt; (also known as Doña Blanca Castle) are also accessible on some trails, adding a historical dimension to your ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Caparroso?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Caparroso are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988469814/caparroso-and-penaflor-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caparroso and Peñaflor Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 39.5 km moderate trail that takes you through the Bardenas Reales and past the historic castle.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Caparroso?
A: The region around Caparroso, particularly the Bardenas Reales, can experience extreme temperatures. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder weather making for a more enjoyable experience. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cold, so plan accordingly.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Caparroso?
A: The mountain bike trails around Caparroso are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Riders frequently praise the unique semi-desert landscapes, the variety of terrain, and the opportunity to explore the Bardenas Reales Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical castles?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988469814/caparroso-and-penaflor-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caparroso and Peñaflor Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you directly to the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3848041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14940673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcilla Castle – Castillo de Peralta loop from Marcilla de Navarra&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to see &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Bardenas Reales?
A: Yes, many routes provide extensive views of the Bardenas Reales. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferial Reservoir – View of the Bardenas Reales loop from El Ferial&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 35.7 km trail specifically designed to showcase the park's unique vistas.

Q: Are there any natural reserves or lakes accessible by mountain bike near Caparroso?
A: While the Bardenas Reales is the primary natural feature, you can find routes that pass by the Ferial Reservoir. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4047253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, known for its birdlife, is also located in the broader region and can be explored as part of a longer ride or separate visit.

Q: What is the difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Caparroso?
A: The trails around Caparroso offer a range of difficulties. You'll find 5 easy routes, 33 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both beginners and experienced mountain bikers can find suitable challenges, from gentle paths to more technical sections requiring good physical condition.
tips: Beautiful views towards the fantastic landscape of the Bardenas Reales natural park, a unique place that by sectors gives the feeling that you are not on this planet!

A magical place that hides a history of romanticism that dates back to the 13th century. Inside the Bardenas Reales park, this tower that has been eroded by the scourge of the weather today is a symbol of the region. Built by Sancho el Fuerte in the 13th century, it was originally conceived to protect the border between Navarre and Aragon. Later it was the prison of Princess Blanca of Navarra who was imprisoned by her father Juan II of Aragon for refusing the agreed marriage. The story goes that every night a shepherd came to give the princess food and drink and that when she was released, as a thank you, she herself gave this shepherd the land where her prison was located.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in Caparroso. You will need to find suitable parking within the town of Caparroso to begin your ride, as specific parking areas for the trailhead are not detailed.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While the Bardenas Reales Natural Park generally allows dogs, they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure safety. Always check the latest park regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Bardenas Reales can get very hot in summer, with limited shade. Spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit, offering more pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Always check weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike in Bardenas Reales Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established routes within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to check the official park website for any updated regulations or restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular mountain bike route, meaning you will start and finish in the same location in Caparroso.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate difficulty. While not overly technical, riders should have good fitness for the approximately 39.5-kilometer duration and some experience with unpaved tracks. Beginners might find the distance and varied terrain challenging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of tracks and trails, typical of the Bardenas Reales. Expect significant portions of gravel and unpaved surfaces. You'll encounter varied conditions, from smooth gravel sections to potentially challenging clay-rich mud if ridden after rainfall. The landscape is undulating, with descents that can be quite engaging.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the unique, semi-desert environment of the Bardenas Reales, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The landscape is characterized by striking rock formations, eroded gorges, and vast plateaus, often described as otherworldly or lunar. It's a truly distinctive natural setting.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the historic Peñaflor Castle (also known as Doña Blanca Castle), a 13th-century ruin with rich history. You'll also pass the Deer Pass, which offers beautiful views of the fantastical landscape.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other trails within the Bardenas Reales. Specifically, it uses parts of the Ronda de Caparroso and the Camino del Sotillo.

faqs.question: What type of bike is recommended for this trail?
faqs.answer: Due to the varied surfaces, which include gravel and unpaved tracks, mountain bikes or gravel bikes with wide tires are highly recommended for this route.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trailhead in Caparroso?
faqs.answer: Caparroso is a town with local services. You should be able to find cafes, pubs, and restaurants for refreshments before or after your ride, as well as accommodation options if you plan to stay overnight.

waypoints.name: Peñaflor Castle (also known as Doña Blanca Castle)
